text: The Hallelujah Mountains of China Zhangjiajie National Forest is one of the most beautiful places in the country, known for its towering quartz sandstone pillar mountains that jut out of the earth. They've become commonly referred to as the Avatar Mountains because of their land inspiration to the highest-grossing film of all time, though they're officially known as the Hallelujah Mountains. While the park's natural beauty draws in over 20,000 visitors every single day, the sheer size of it allows for some peace and quiet in various corners of the park, which we gladly took upon ourselves to explore. The National Forest is split into five different sections, Yuanjiajie, Yangjiajie, Qianzi Mountain, Huangshi Village, and the Golden Whip Stream. In this video, we'll take you on our journey exploring all five areas, showing you the amazing sights you'll get to see, as well as our travel guide with key pieces of information. We made it through security and right now we're just waiting a few minutes until 7am when these doors officially open for our tickets. Now, if you have not bought them online on trip.com or any other source, you could buy them right here at the entrance. Do note though, during high season, that line is going to be in sync. Now, one way that we've noticed when we first got checked into the hotel, and this is by far the best way to book your tickets, okay guys, book it with your hotel. Not only do you get a discount, you also get a crazy good deal on the cable cars as well as the elevator. So, our tickets in total were 210 yuan for the entrance and 248 yuan for unlimited rides on all the cable cars as well as the elevator. So, across four days, everything for us is included and that by far is the greatest deal. After scanning our tickets at the east entrance, we headed towards the first area we would be exploring, Yuanjiajie, famous for the Avatar or Hallelujah Mountains. To get around the park, you will need to take a bus that shuttles you around the different areas and thankfully, all bus routes are included with your park entrance ticket. Our first stop was the record-breaking Bailong Elevator. It looks insane from this view just seeing how tall it is and the fact that there's three different lanes and by the way, each elevator is actually double-decker. So, each elevator space has space from the top as well as space from the bottom. Also, down here, we get a first look at these insane mountains. This is so cool. We're not even at the Avatar Mountains yet, but it already looks like we're seeing it right now. After getting your tickets and registering your passport, which is something you have to do in order to get into the park, all you have to do is scan your face at every entrance. So, no need to bring out your tickets, no need to bring out your passports, just bring your face and then you'll pass through. Oh my goodness, are we on a movie set? Where is this real life? So, this pillar right here behind me is the hero of the entire Avatar Mountains because it is so narrow at the base and it rises by itself jutting through the earth all the way up to the skies. It is absolutely unbelievable how beautiful this pillar is. There's no wonder there's so many people over here trying to capture the same thing. We actually didn't grab breakfast before, so we're so glad. It's very easy to grab food throughout the entire park. There are so many street stalls here and it feels like we're just strolling through a morning Chinese market. So many fruits, fruit juices, but all the fresh food here is definitely calling my name and we decided to get some hot, this crispy, savory tofu. We got a couple of pancakes. One is a flatter like egg crepe and then the other one is more of like a neat green onion pancake. It's so savory. There's a lot of like chili oil, sesame seeds on the outside and the crunch is definitely from some cornstarch and they grill the tofu. So on the inside it's so soft, kind of milky and the outside nice and crunchy, salty and it has a bit of a Sichuan flavor. After quite a bit of walking, we made it to the first bridge under heaven. This is a completely natural bridge and creates a gorgeous archway between the mountains. This is the final spot in the Yuanjiajie area and then we're going to head on to a bus before heading to the next section of the park. So we just overheard one of the tour guides saying that this bridge is 350 meters in elevation compared to the ground below. That is crazy. I can't imagine walking on it before there was any barrier. We've just made it to our next section which is called Yangjiajie. Now do not get it confused with Yuanjiajie. That is where the Avatar Mountains are. Over here it is significantly quieter. Tour groups don't really come here because honestly the views aren't the best but we're still excited to explore especially because we have a lot of time here. If you are short on time, I probably wouldn't recommend coming to this area. There are two main things we're doing. One, we're going through these woods to get to a viewpoint and number two, because of our unlimited cableway pass, we're going to ride the cable car up and down so we can enjoy the views along the way. Our flat paved path with some stairs is now done and now we have to take on this challenge over here. The left is where we go up and then the right ladder is where we go down. The left one's a little bit steeper so we're going to take the dangerous path first. That is significantly steeper. That's actually pretty wild when you look at it. Yeah that's why it's safer to go up it. At the very top it's a near vertical. This is actually pretty sketch. Yeah I feel like when you have the dome part it's hard to keep sliding your hands over the bar. Oh this is so awkward. So so Thanks to our limited rides pass, we decided to hop on the Yangjiajie cable car for a little round trip scenic journey. One of the best ways to enjoy the incredible mountain views is from the cableway and for about 15 minutes we got to sit back and do just that. Well that was a very aggressive bus ride. Our driver did not fear death. It felt like he was a Formula One driver. He's just ripping through those corners at the top of the mountain on speeds I've never seen. But we have made it safely over to Tianzi mountain and over here is a whole different section of the park and arguably this is what could be considered the second most popular after Yangjiajie where the Avatar mountains are. We don't really know what's in store for us here but based on the popularity the views should be great. We were heading towards the scenic area but as we were leaving the bus we noticed that there was this massive alley filled with a bunch of different food stalls. So we decided it was time for lunch because it is 2 p.m. so we got some massive squid skewer which is 20 yuan. Pretty ridiculous how affordable that was. This one was the same price and it's basically like grilled potatoes. Those wedges do look really good though. Mouth is salivating a little bit and the oil on the skewers is dripping as I'm talking right now so I think I'm just gonna have to go and dig in. So with three sections of the park checked off it was time to ride the cable car back down and get ready for day two. So good morning guys it is day two of us exploring the Zhangjiajie National Forest. Today we're actually going to be heading into a different gate into the park. I believe there's like five different gates that you can head into. Today we're going to go through the southern gate and now to get there we can't just go through the same entrance obviously that we went through yesterday. We actually need to go catch a bus that should be about 15 to 20 minutes. I'll take you to the southern gate and it should also cost 10 yuan per person. Unfortunately this is where taking the bus didn't make a whole lot of sense. Yes we did need to enter through the southern gate but the bus doesn't leave until it's full which meant we waited a good 20 minutes until it was. It turns out that if you use DD, China's ride share app, it's actually the same cost if you're already a group of two. When we got into the park we were met with stunning views and a ton of monkeys. We hopped on the inner park shuttle bus to the cable car where we visited the fourth area of the park, Huangshi village. It turns out that Huangshi village is a lot higher than we had first thought and the cable car took us quite high up in elevation. Now it's so much more beautiful than we had expected which is such a surprise considering it's so much less visited and we read online before that the abattoir mountains can only be found in the Renjiajie area but that doesn't really make sense to us anymore because we've been seeing them throughout the entire park and here in Huangshi village they're right up next to you just like we've seen them before. This area takes about two to three hours to complete. It essentially creates a full loop with a bunch of different viewpoints along the way and hopefully we won't be encountering too many stairs up and down as our legs are killing us from the last couple of days. There are actually two different loops that you can take over here. There's the grand loop as Lauren mentioned that one takes about two hours in length. Now there is a smaller loop to take if you're short on time. That one should reduce your time by at least half so this way if you didn't want to spend your entire day up here on this village you can go ahead and take the shorter route but you still get all of the major viewpoints. So the fun thing about Huangshi is how many monkeys there are. You do have to be careful though because they can be aggressive as we're looking for food so don't have any plastic bags out, any little snacks out. Wait until you're done the loop before you start eating otherwise one of these guys is gonna try to grab it. We took the cable car back down and then took the same bus that took us to the cable car from the southern entrance and from here we will begin the fifth and final section of the national forest. So don't forget we went to four already. Yuanjiajie, Yangjiajie, Tianzi mountain and then Huangshi village earlier this morning. So this area is called the golden whip stream and here we're going to walk along the base of the mountains and you can see the soaring towers all above us. It's actually a really peaceful walk because not too many people come over here. A lot of the tour groups kind of avoid it because it is a two-hour trek in order to get to the end. Either way we're gonna enjoy it. It's so peaceful and quiet. Without the crowds this peaceful walk was honestly very enjoyable for Jason and me. With the sound of the stream to our right and the towering mountains ahead of us time flew by. One thing to note is the crazy number of monkeys that call this area home. Be careful because if they see you with food they will not be shy to grab it right out of your hands. So we're pretty hungry walking the trail. It's also taking a little bit longer than we had expected. So we're here for a little lunch stop. Over here we got two huge lamb skewers as well as a little egg pancake. This was quite similar to the one we had yesterday and these two were only about 15 yuan each so not too bad. We finally finished our walk along the golden whip stream and it did feel like it was a lot longer than we had expected. Well it was two and a half hours including our lunch break. It wasn't strenuous but we definitely recommend that you only do it if you have the time and if you want to see the monkeys but we did see plenty up in Huangshui. It seems like our time in the national forest is just about complete but there is one other area that a few maps will show as a sick section called the 10 mile gallery. It's a rather dull walk in our opinion that is in fact not 10 miles long but only two kilometers. If you didn't want to walk you could take the small train that runs alongside the path which seems better suited for families. The views are just okay especially after seeing the other areas of the park which makes this area perfectly fine to skip in our opinion. With that said we hope you guys enjoyed this travel guide on Zhangjiajie and we'll catch you on our next adventure.
